findings.title: Cobalt Strike SMB Beacon via WMI Lateral Movement
findings.description: A Cobalt Strike SMB Beacon was deployed on BASE-WKSTN-05 via WMI remote execution. Three WMI-spawned PowerShell chains used encoded commands with gzip/base64 shellcode and Wow64 RunAs32 injection to establish a reflective DLL loader communicating over named pipe \\.\ pipe\diagsvc-22.

findings.title: Post-Exploitation via Rundll32 Injection
findings.description: PowerShell PID 1332 spawned 5 rundll32.exe instances for DLL injection between 01:31 and 21:07 on Aug 31, 2018. All instances exited after brief execution, consistent with code injection or task execution via proxy binary.

findings.title: Persistent Orphaned Rundll32 Beacon
findings.description: Rundll32.exe PID 7100 ran for 6+ days with parent PID 7148 absent from the process list. 5 threads and 337 handles indicate active execution, likely a persistent Cobalt Strike beacon.

findings.title: AV and Monitoring Evasion
findings.description: The attack succeeded despite McAfee Endpoint Security (8 processes) and Sysmon64 with Cobalt Strike detection rules being active. Reflective injection and in-memory execution bypassed signature-based detection.

findings.title: ACH: Targeted APT Most Plausible
findings.description: Analysis of Competing Hypotheses evaluated 5 explanations against 7 evidence items. Targeted APT intrusion (H1) had 0 inconsistencies. Commodity malware (3), insider threat (4), and legitimate activity (6) were eliminated. Red team (1 inconsistency) was eliminated by scenario context.
